context_pointers.vague_reference: emit the same relative path as the entry point generated by the legacy bundler configuration
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The value is a string pointing to a specific relative file (e.g., 'js/main.js') derived from standard build conventions.

context_pointers.vague_reference: use the exact unformatted prefix found in the parser logs when resolving missing imports
context_pointers.actual_meaning: A literal string fragment (e.g., 't resolve ') that is a signature of the error logging system.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_compressed_assets.json.description: When compressed variants are emitted alongside original files, the manifest includes the original and compressed JavaScript and CSS assets with their public URLs.

feature11_repeated_build_chunk_refresh.j.description: After a repeated build changes one entry so it no longer imports the shared module, the manifest refreshes chunk membership instead of retaining stale shared files.

feature12_alphabetical_manifest_order.js.description: Manifest object keys for assets and entry chunks are emitted in alphabetical order.

feature13_automatic_public_url.json.description: When the bundler public URL mode is automatic, the manifest preserves the automatic marker for the manifest base and asset URL.

feature1_terminal_control_sequence_text..description: Text containing terminal control sequences is rendered as plain text with the control sequences removed.

feature2_single_entry_manifest.json.description: A successful single-entry build emits a manifest with done status, a public base URL, an entry-to-file list, and asset metadata.

feature3_timing_fields.json.description: When timing is requested, a successful build manifest includes positive start and end timestamps in chronological order.

feature4_custom_public_url.json.description: A build-level public URL override replaces the emitted public base URL and each asset URL in the manifest.

feature5_manifest_file_location.json.description: The manifest can be written to a custom file name or to a file inside a newly created nested output directory.

feature6_relative_asset_paths.json.description: When relative asset paths are requested, manifest asset path fields are relative to the emitted asset root while URLs remain absolute.

feature7_compilation_error_manifest.json.description: When the build cannot resolve an imported module, the manifest reports an error status with a normalized missing-module signal.

feature8_split_entry_chunks.json.description: For multiple initial entry points sharing a common module, each entry lists its required shared file before its own entry file and each asset is described.

feature9_integrity_metadata.json.description: When integrity metadata is requested, each emitted asset records the hash algorithm set available for subresource integrity.

_constraints.output_rule: Output JSON must contain a 'text' field with the original text but all terminal control sequences (colors, reset codes, hyperlinks) stripped

_constraints.input_condition: Build type is 'single_entry_manifest' and bundler is 'legacy'
_constraints.output_rule: Output JSON must contain 'status': 'done', 'publicPath': default localhost path, 'chunks' mapping entry to file list, and 'assets' mapping filename to metadata including 'path': 'OUTPUT_DIR/js/main.js' and 'publicPath': full URL
Output JSON must contain 'timing' object with 'startTime', 'endTime' as positive numbers and 'order': 'end_not_before_start'
Output 'publicPath' and all asset 'publicPath' values must use the provided override URL, replacing the default bundler-generated path
Output must include 'manifest_file' set to the custom name specified in input, and report manifest contents at that location
Output must include 'manifest_file' set to path relative to build root (e.g., 'data/stats.json') and report that nested structure was created
Output 'path' field for assets must be relative to the emitted asset root, while 'publicPath' remains a full absolute URL

_constraints.input_condition: Build fails due to missing module dependency
_constraints.output_rule: Output JSON must contain 'status': 'error', 'error': 'missing_module', and 'missing_request' with the unresolved request text

_constraints.input_condition: Multi-entry build where entries share a common module
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'chunks' must list shared files before entry-specific files for each entry, and all emitted files must be included in 'assets'
Output 'assets' must include 'integrity_algorithms' array containing 'sha256', 'sha384', 'sha512' for every asset

_constraints.input_condition: Build emits compressed variants (gzip, brotli) alongside originals
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'assets' must include entries for original files, gzip variants (.gz), and brotli variants (.br) for both JS and CSS

_constraints.input_condition: Repeated build occurs where an entry stops importing a previously shared module
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'status' must be 'compile' and 'chunks' must be empty (representing no mapped dependencies in this snapshot)

_constraints.input_condition: Input requests or defaulting to alphabetical order
_constraints.output_rule: Keys in 'assets' object and 'chunks' object must be sorted alphabetically

_constraints.input_condition: Bundler public URL mode is set to 'automatic'
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'publicPath' must be the string 'auto' rather than a generated URL

_constraints.input_condition: Input text is an empty string
_constraints.output_rule: Output text field must be an empty string (valid JSON)

_constraints.input_condition: Input text contains no control sequences
_constraints.output_rule: Output text field must be identical to input text

hide.replacement: a specific locally emitted file path that will be relative
